theme: Excite an antenna array to shape its beam
title: Phase-Only Beam Shaping on a Linear Array
seed_problem: phase-only array-factor pattern synthesis with sidelobe-null placement
mutation: composite: pattern-matching objective (array-factor-synthesis) coupled to a hard non-linear sidelobe-null penalty (sidelobe-null-placement), both evaluated under a fixed-magnitude phase-only aperture constraint (phase-only-constraint) so the excitation search space is a non-convex product of circles, not a free complex vector.
strategies: trivial: do-nothing uniform phasing phi_i=0 for all elements (reproduces the checker baseline)
naive greedy: steer a single conventional beam at the single strongest target angle (textbook phased-array steering), ignoring secondary lobes, the rest of the pattern shape, and all null constraints

insight (strong): alternating projection between the desired complex far-field (replace magnitude by the target/threshold-consistent value, keep the current phase) and the fixed-magnitude phase-only aperture (adjoint back-projection then re-project each element's excitation onto its fixed-magnitude circle), with adaptive reweighting of violated null angles across iterations
innovation_hook: Phase-only synthesis is a projection problem -- alternate between the desired pattern and the fixed-magnitude aperture constraint rather than inverting once.
